notes: Atomic propositions are schematic and parameterized by the verifier.
Parameterized APs are marked with 'parameterized': true — they are implicitly quantified over an object or grasper ID and cannot be treated as ground propositions.
Effect rules state what becomes true in the next state; they do not encode frame conditions (unchanged fluents). A closed-world assumption over fluents is required for a complete verifier.

Two API layers are distinguished: 'env.*' rules apply at the ShrdluBlocksEnv.execute_action boundary; 'ctrl.*' rules apply at the Controller method boundary. The env layer is stricter on argument types.
The X (next) operator always references the same base AP names as the current-state vocabulary; no shadow 'next_*' propositions are used.

atomic_propositions.name: move_requested
atomic_propositions.description: A move_grasper action was issued this step.

atomic_propositions.name: lower_requested
atomic_propositions.description: A lower_grasper action was issued this step.

atomic_propositions.name: raise_requested
atomic_propositions.description: A raise_grasper action was issued this step.

atomic_propositions.name: close_requested
atomic_propositions.description: A close_grasper action was issued this step.

atomic_propositions.name: open_requested
atomic_propositions.description: A open_grasper action was issued this step.

atomic_propositions.name: highlight_requested
atomic_propositions.description: A highlight_object action was issued this step.

atomic_propositions.name: unhighlight_requested
atomic_propositions.description: An unhighlight_object action was issued this step.

atomic_propositions.name: explicit_grasper_id_supplied
atomic_propositions.description: The caller passed an explicit grasper_id argument (not relying on the default).

atomic_propositions.name: grasper_exists
atomic_propositions.description: At least one grasper object exists in the scene (or a valid default grasper is set).

atomic_propositions.name: object_exists
atomic_propositions.description: The referenced obj_id corresponds to an existing object in the scene.

atomic_propositions.name: referenced_object_is_grasper
atomic_propositions.description: The explicitly supplied grasper_id refers to an object whose kind tag is 'grasper'.

atomic_propositions.name: lowered
atomic_propositions.description: The active grasper's 'lowered' tag is True.

atomic_propositions.name: closed
atomic_propositions.description: The active grasper's 'closed' tag is True.

atomic_propositions.name: holding
atomic_propositions.description: The active grasper's 'grasped' tag is non-None (it holds an object).

atomic_propositions.name: has_numeric_x
atomic_propositions.description: The x argument to move_grasper is an int or float (not None, not a non-numeric type).

atomic_propositions.name: has_numeric_y
atomic_propositions.description: The y argument to move_grasper is an int or float (not None, not a non-numeric type).

atomic_propositions.name: has_integer_obj_id
atomic_propositions.description: The obj_id argument is a Python int.

atomic_propositions.name: target_x_in_bounds
atomic_propositions.description: The x argument (if given) satisfies min_x <= x <= max_x for the grasper.

atomic_propositions.name: target_y_in_bounds
atomic_propositions.description: The y argument (if given) satisfies min_y <= y <= max_y for the grasper.

atomic_propositions.name: grasper_centered_over_object
atomic_propositions.description: The horizontal distance from the grasper position to the target object's center of mass is within grasper_distance_tolerance. Only when this holds is grasper.tags['resting_on'] set after lowering.

atomic_propositions.name: grasper_resting_on_graspable
atomic_propositions.description: After lowering, the grasper's 'resting_on' tag is set AND the object it names has graspable=True.

atomic_propositions.name: target_graspable
atomic_propositions.description: The object directly below the grasper has graspable=True in its tags.

atomic_propositions.name: held_obj_resting_on_target
atomic_propositions.description: The held object's own 'resting_on' tag is non-None (set when the held object was lowered onto a supporting surface).

atomic_propositions.name: target_can_support_held
atomic_propositions.description: can_support(target, held_obj) is True: target has can_support=True AND (for boxes) all salient points of the held object fall within the box footprint.

atomic_propositions.name: held_object_xy_matches_grasper_xy
atomic_propositions.description: After a move, the held object's (x, y) equals the grasper's (x, y).

atomic_propositions.name: grasper_clears_scene
atomic_propositions.description: After raising, the grasper (and any held object) is at a z-height strictly above the highest stable point in the scene.

atomic_propositions.name: grasper_resting_on_is_null
atomic_propositions.description: The grasper's 'resting_on' tag is None.

atomic_propositions.name: held_obj_resting_on_is_null
atomic_propositions.description: The held object's 'resting_on' tag is None (cleared after raising).

atomic_propositions.name: grasper_lowered_onto_highest_below_or_ground
atomic_propositions.description: After lowering, the grasper (or held object base) z-coordinate equals the height of the highest object directly below, or 0 if none.

rules.natural_language: At the env API layer, move_grasper requires both x and y to be numeric (int or float). Both are mandatory; neither may be omitted or non-numeric.
At the controller layer, move_grasper requires at least one of x or y to be non-None. (Both being None is an error; one may be None and takes its current value.)
notes: This is weaker than the env-layer rule. The env layer always passes both; the controller alone accepts one.

rules.natural_language: highlight_object and unhighlight_object require an integer obj_id at the env API layer.
Any grasper action (move, lower, raise, close, open) requires a valid grasper to be present in the scene.
If an explicit grasper_id is supplied, the referenced object must have kind='grasper'.
highlight_object and unhighlight_object require the referenced obj_id to correspond to an existing object.
move_grasper requires the grasper to not be lowered.
If x is specified for move_grasper, it must lie within the grasper's [min_x, max_x] bounds.
If y is specified for move_grasper, it must lie within the grasper's [min_y, max_y] bounds.
lower_grasper requires the grasper to currently be raised (not lowered).
raise_grasper requires the grasper to currently be lowered.
close_grasper requires the grasper to currently be open (not closed).
open_grasper requires the grasper to currently be closed.
If open_grasper is called while holding an object, the grasper must be lowered first.
If opening while holding, the HELD OBJECT's own 'resting_on' tag must be non-None — i.e., the held object (not the grasper) must be resting on a surface.
notes: This checks grasped_obj.tags['resting_on'], not grasper.tags['resting_on']. The two are distinct. The previous version used the ambiguous 'resting_on_target' AP which did not make this distinction.

rules.natural_language: If opening while holding, the surface the held object is resting on must be able to support it: can_support=True and (for boxes) all salient points of the held object must fall within the box footprint.
notes: target_can_support_held encodes scenes.py:85-96: can_support=True AND, for box targets, all salient points of the held object lie inside the box footprint (not just the center of mass).
Pyramids always have can_support=False and can never be drop targets.
The box has a stricter full-footprint check; plain blocks only check whether the held object's center of mass is within the target's footprint.

rules.natural_language: Closing while lowered and centered can only begin grasping if the object below has graspable=True. Objects with graspable=False (e.g. the box, the table) cannot be grasped.
notes: Reads as: if close while lowered, then either the object below is not graspable (in which case grasping simply does not start) or the next state has holding=True.
A close on a non-graspable object still succeeds mechanically — it just does not begin grasping. The closed state is set regardless.

rules.natural_language: Even if the grasper is lowered onto a graspable object, grasping only succeeds if the horizontal distance from the grasper to the object's center of mass is within grasper_distance_tolerance. Only then is grasper.tags['resting_on'] set, which is the gate for the grasp.
notes: grasper_centered_over_object is a geometry AP: horizontal_distance(grasper.position, object.position) <= grasper_distance_tolerance.
If not centered, grasper.tags['resting_on'] is None even though a graspable object is below, so grasping does not begin.

rules.natural_language: Any successful close_grasper call sets closed=True in the next state, regardless of whether grasping occurs.
Closing while lowered, centered over a graspable object (resting_on tag is set) yields closed=True and holding=True in the next state.
notes: This is a refinement of effect.close_always_sets_closed: the grasp subcase.
grasper_resting_on_graspable is the conjunction of grasper.tags['resting_on'] being non-None AND the named object having graspable=True — both conditions in control.py:84-87.

rules.natural_language: Opening while closed, lowered, holding, and with the held object resting on a supporting surface releases the object: next state has closed=False and holding=False.
Opening a closed but empty grasper (not holding) sets closed=False in the next state.
lower_grasper sets lowered=True in the next state.
After lowering (empty grasper), the grasper's z-coordinate equals the height of the highest object directly below it, or 0 if none exists.
notes: When not holding, the grasper's position is set to (x, y, target_height) where target_height is the highest point of the object directly below, or 0.

rules.natural_language: After lowering while holding, the held object's base z-coordinate equals the height of the highest object directly below the grasper, or 0 if none.
notes: The grasper's z is set to target_height + height_of_held_object; the held object's z is set to target_height.
The held object acquires resting_on = target only if target is non-None. If lowered over empty space, resting_on stays None.

rules.natural_language: lower_grasper only sets the grasper's (or held object's) 'resting_on' tag if the grasper is horizontally centered over the object below within grasper_distance_tolerance. If not centered, 'resting_on' remains None even though the grasper has physically descended.
notes: Equivalently: NOT grasper_centered_over_object → X(grasper_resting_on_is_null).
This distinction matters for close_grasper: if resting_on is not set, closing while lowered will not begin grasping even if a graspable object is physically below.

rules.natural_language: raise_grasper sets lowered=False in the next state.
raise_grasper positions the grasper (and any held object) at a height strictly above the highest stable point in the scene.
notes: Stable height is computed over all objects excluding the grasper itself and objects currently grasped (control.py:347).
The grasper z is set to minimum_height = max_stable_z + 0.1. When holding, the held object base is at minimum_height and the grasper is at the held object's highest point.

rules.natural_language: raise_grasper sets grasper.tags['resting_on'] = None in the next state.
If raise_grasper is called while holding an object, that object's 'resting_on' tag is also cleared to None in the next state.
Moving while holding transports the held object so that its (x, y) matches the grasper's (x, y) in the next state.
notes: The held object's z is preserved as grasper.position - Point(0, 0, height_offset), keeping the same vertical distance to the grasper.

clean_ast_fits: All state-toggle preconditions (lower/raise/close/open require correct current state)
Argument type checks (numeric x/y, integer obj_id)
Single-step effect rules (lower sets lowered, raise clears lowered, close sets closed)
Raise clears resting_on for grasper and held object
abstracted_ast_fits: Support checks for dropping (target_can_support_held encodes the box-specific footprint rule)
Move bounds checks (per-axis, conditioned on argument presence)
Lower rests on highest object below (grasper_lowered_onto_highest_below_or_ground)
Centering tolerance for resting_on tag assignment (grasper_centered_over_object)
known_omissions: Frame conditions: rules do not state which fluents are unchanged by each action. A closed-world assumption is required.
Exact numeric geometry (grasper position z-values, bounding box arithmetic) is encoded in abstract APs rather than arithmetic LTL.
The default_grasper selection logic (first grasper found in scene if none set) is not encoded.
recommended_usage: Bind each AP from real or predicted simulator state at each step.
Evaluate geometry-heavy APs (grasper_centered_over_object, grasper_clears_scene, target_can_support_held) via Python-computed predicates rather than symbolic expansion.
Apply frame axioms externally: assume each unmentioned fluent is unchanged unless an effect rule explicitly sets it.
Distinguish env-layer rules (mandatory for agent-facing API) from ctrl-layer rules (apply when calling Controller directly).
